example.p: Brief: B2B SaaS dashboard with role-gated content editing, ~200 editors, REST API for a small set of mobile clients. Team: 4 Python developers, mixed familiarity. Throughput floor: 100 RPS.
example.code: { "framework": "django", "triggered_by_rule": "r1-django-when-admin", "rationale": "Editor admin is the dominant cost driver. Django admin + permissions + auth save the equivalent of 1-2 sprints versus building it on FastAPI.", "non_functional_drivers": ["admin UI", "role-gated permissions", "relational ORM coverage"], "downstream_methodologies": ["django-base-model", "django-models", "django-api", "django-coding-standards", "django-pytest"], "adr_line": "framework = django" }

example.p: Brief: HTTP gateway in front of an LLM provider with streaming responses, WebSocket fanout, no admin UI, no relational ORM. Team: 2 senior Python developers comfortable with asyncio. Throughput floor: 800 concurrent streams.
example.code: { "framework": "fastapi", "triggered_by_rule": "r2-fastapi-when-async", "rationale": "WebSocket fanout + streaming LLM responses demand a native-async stack. OpenAPI auto-generation also pays the docs cost down.", "non_functional_drivers": ["native async I/O", "WebSocket fanout", "auto OpenAPI"], "downstream_methodologies": ["python-fastapi", "python-async", "python-typing"], "adr_line": "framework = fastapi" }

example.p: Brief: One-page web hook receiver that queues a Celery job. No admin. No auth (private network). Throughput floor: 5 RPS. Lifetime: ~6 months until pipeline rewrite.
Decision tree → admin_needed=false, async_needs=false, scope=small → conclusion `r3-flask-when-small`.
example.code: { "framework": "flask", "triggered_by_rule": "r3-flask-when-small", "rationale": "Truly small surface, short lifetime, no admin, no async; full Django/FastAPI ceremony would be overkill.", "non_functional_drivers": ["minimal core", "single endpoint", "<200 LOC expected"], "downstream_methodologies": ["python-typing"], "adr_line": "framework = flask" }
